name: Two Prakritis — Para and Apara
proposition: The divine has two natures: the lower eightfold material nature and the higher living nature (jivabhuta) that sustains the world
repetition_rationale: First statement of the two-nature doctrine. Ch9 and Ch13 develop it further.
echo_hypothesis: Search for para/apara prakrti distinction in Sankhya passages, Moksha-dharma of Shantiparva

name: The Divine as Origin and Dissolution of All
proposition: Both natures together are the womb of all beings; the divine is the origin (prabhava) and the dissolution (pralaya) of the entire world
repetition_rationale: Stated again at 9.18, 10.8, 14.4 — BG insists that all emergence and dissolution trace to the one source
echo_hypothesis: Cosmogonic passages in Adiparva, Shanti-parva creation hymns

name: Nothing Higher Than the Divine — Beads on a Thread
proposition: There is nothing higher than the divine; all of this is strung on it as beads on a thread
repetition_rationale: Repeated in essence at 9.4-5 and 10.42. The string-of-beads metaphor is distinctive.
echo_hypothesis: String/thread metaphors for cosmic unity in MBH philosophical passages

name: The Divine Is the Essence in All Things
proposition: The divine is the taste in water, the light of sun and moon, the sacred syllable in the Vedas, sound in space, the vital force in all beings
repetition_rationale: Anticipates the vibhuti catalogue of ch10; here stated as inner essence (rasa) not outward glory
echo_hypothesis: Immanence language — divinity as essence of natural phenomena — in MBH hymns

name: The Three Gunas Originate from the Divine — But the Divine Transcends Them
proposition: The three-guna states all arise from the divine, yet the divine is not in them; the world, deluded by these three, does not know the divine
repetition_rationale: The asymmetric relation: gunas in the divine, not divine in gunas. Restated in 9.4-5.
echo_hypothesis: Passages distinguishing the absolute from guna-bound existence in Shantiparva Mokshadharma

name: Maya as Divine and Difficult to Cross
proposition: This divine maya made of gunas is difficult to cross; only those who take refuge in the divine cross beyond it
repetition_rationale: Maya as divine (not inferior) power — repeated at 18.61. Only surrender crosses it.
echo_hypothesis: Maya as divine delusion in Shantiparva, Indra-maya episodes in Adiparva

name: Four Types of Devotees — Arta Jijnasu Artharthi Jnani
proposition: Four kinds worship the divine: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of benefit, and the wise; the wise is highest
repetition_rationale: Typology of seekers unique to ch7. The jnani-as-atman-of-God claim (7.18) is the most radical.
echo_hypothesis: Typologies of seekers in MBH — distressed devotees, philosophers, pragmatic worshippers

name: Vasudeva Is All — Rare Knowledge
proposition: After many births, the wise one who knows that Vasudeva is all takes refuge — such a great soul is exceedingly rare
repetition_rationale: The ekam sat proposition made explicit — vAsudevaH sarvam. The theological nucleus of the project.
echo_hypothesis: Any passage where a sage declares the unity of all in one divine principle

name: Worship of Other Deities — Limited but Valid
proposition: Those whose knowledge is stolen by desires worship other gods, but even that worship reaches the divine — though the fruits are limited
repetition_rationale: Restated at 9.23-24. All worship is redirected to the one — with a soteriological hierarchy.
echo_hypothesis: Debates on relative merit of worshipping different deities in MBH

name: The Divine Conceals Itself Through Yoga-Maya
proposition: The divine is not manifest to all, veiled by yoga-maya; the deluded world does not recognize the divine as unborn and imperishable
repetition_rationale: The epistemology of concealment — why the divine is not universally recognized.
echo_hypothesis: Passages on divine concealment, disguise episodes in MBH

name: Duality-Delusion Veils All Beings at Birth
proposition: All beings are born into the delusion of duality arising from desire and aversion
repetition_rationale: The structural diagnosis of bondage: dvandva-moha at the moment of birth.
echo_hypothesis: Birth-bondage passages, desire/aversion as the root of samsara in Shantiparva

name: Aksara Brahman — the Imperishable Supreme
proposition: The imperishable (akshara) is the supreme Brahman; its own nature is called the adhyatma; the creative impulse that produces beings is called karma
repetition_rationale: The technical vocabulary of ch8 — defining terms for cosmic levels.
echo_hypothesis: Akshara Brahman as technical term in Upanishad-influenced sections of Shantiparva

name: Last Thought at Death Determines Rebirth
proposition: Whatever state of being one remembers at the moment of death, to that state alone one goes
repetition_rationale: The death-moment doctrine is repeated at 8.13 for the specific case of remembering the divine.
echo_hypothesis: Death-moment teachings in Shantiparva, instructions to dying warriors in Drona/Karna parvas

name: OM as the Imperishable — Dying While Remembering
proposition: Uttering the single syllable OM, which is Brahman, remembering the divine, one who departs attains the highest goal
repetition_rationale: OM as the sonic form of the imperishable — specific yogic instruction at death.
echo_hypothesis: OM as salvific sound in MBH — dying sages who utter OM

name: The Great-Souled Do Not Return — Moksha as Non-Return
proposition: Having attained the highest perfection, the great-souled ones do not take rebirth in this impermanent home of suffering
repetition_rationale: The negative definition of liberation: non-return. Contrasted with all worlds up to Brahmaloka being subject to return.
echo_hypothesis: Non-return teachings in Shantiparva Mokshadharma

name: Cyclic Cosmic Time — Brahma's Day and Night
proposition: Brahma's day spans a thousand yugas; his night another thousand; at dawn all beings emerge from the unmanifest, at nightfall they dissolve back
repetition_rationale: The kalpa-yuga cosmology — unique to ch8. Establishes cosmic time scale.
echo_hypothesis: Yuga-cycle cosmology passages in Shantiparva, Adiparva creation narratives, Markandeya's teachings

name: Beyond the Unmanifest — The Eternal Unperishing Abode
proposition: Beyond the unmanifest there is another eternal unmanifest which does not perish when all beings perish — this is the highest goal
repetition_rationale: The metaphysical stratification: manifest -> unmanifest -> beyond-unmanifest.
echo_hypothesis: Three-level ontology passages in Shantiparva — vyakta/avyakta/akshara discussions

name: The Two Paths — Light and Dark (Uttarayana and Daksinayana)
proposition: Fire, light, day, bright fortnight, northern sun — departing this way one goes to Brahman; smoke, night, dark fortnight, southern sun — one returns
repetition_rationale: The two-paths doctrine (devayana/pitriyana) is ancient Vedic — BG appropriates it.
echo_hypothesis: Devayana/pitriyana passages in Shantiparva and cosmological discourse

name: Rajavidya — The Royal Knowledge, Royal Secret
proposition: This knowledge is the royal knowledge, the royal secret, supremely purifying, directly known by experience
repetition_rationale: The self-designating claim of the BG as supreme knowledge.
echo_hypothesis: Brahmavidya / rajavidya language in Shantiparva

name: God Pervades All Yet Does Not Dwell in Beings — Cosmic Paradox
proposition: All beings subsist in the divine yet the divine does not dwell in them — the great wind dwells in space yet space is not in the wind
repetition_rationale: The simultaneous immanence and transcendence paradox.
echo_hypothesis: Philosophical passages on immanence without dependence in Shantiparva

name: God as Cosmic Creator Through Prakriti — Unmoved Mover
proposition: At the end of each kalpa all beings flow back into prakriti; at the beginning the divine sends them forth again — seated like an indifferent witness
repetition_rationale: God as unmoved first mover of cosmic cycles.
echo_hypothesis: Passages on the unmoved mover of cosmic cycles in Shantiparva

name: The Foolish Mistake the Human Form
proposition: The deluded disregard the divine when it takes human form, not knowing its higher nature
repetition_rationale: The avatar-recognition problem — crucial for MBH narrative.
echo_hypothesis: Episodes where Krishna's divinity is unrecognized — Duryodhana's rejection, Shishupala

name: The Divine Is All Cosmic Relations
proposition: The divine is father, mother, sustainer, grandfather, the way, supporter, lord, witness, dwelling, refuge, friend, origin, dissolution, ground, and imperishable seed
repetition_rationale: The total-relation litany: every possible relational category is claimed.
echo_hypothesis: Prayers and hymns to the divine as all-relations in MBH

name: Svarga Through Ritual Is Temporary
proposition: Those who worship through Vedic ritual attain heaven — but when merit is exhausted they return to the mortal world
repetition_rationale: The critique of svarga-seeking religion: effective but impermanent.
echo_hypothesis: Critiques of svarga-seeking in Shantiparva, Markandeya's discourses

name: Ananya Bhakti — Undivided Devotion, Divine Provides
proposition: Those who worship with undivided attention — the divine carries what they lack and preserves what they have
repetition_rationale: Yoga-kshema as divine promise to undivided devotees.
echo_hypothesis: Divine protection of devotees in MBH — Draupadi's rescue, Prahlada

name: All Worship Reaches the Divine — One Recipient Behind All Gods
proposition: Even those who worship other gods with faith worship the divine alone, though by an improper method
repetition_rationale: All yajna has one true recipient. Foundational for the ekam sat thesis.
echo_hypothesis: Universalist religious passages in MBH — Bhishma's teaching on one god behind many names

name: Even a Leaf Offered with Devotion Is Accepted
proposition: Whoever offers a leaf, flower, fruit, or water with devotion — the divine accepts that gift
repetition_rationale: The democratization of worship — any offering with pure devotion is received.
echo_hypothesis: Stories of humble offerings accepted by the divine in MBH

name: Offer All Actions to the Divine — Madarpana
proposition: Whatever you do, eat, offer, give — do that as an offering to the divine; thus freed from karma-bonds
repetition_rationale: Karma-yoga distilled to its simplest: all action as divine offering.
echo_hypothesis: Arpana language in MBH — any sage teaching that all action should be dedicated to the divine

name: Equal Vision — The Divine Is the Same Toward All
proposition: The divine is the same toward all beings; no one is hateful or dear — but those who worship with devotion are in the divine, and the divine is in them
repetition_rationale: Divine impartiality as baseline — but devotion creates reciprocal inhabitation.
echo_hypothesis: Teachings on divine impartiality in Shantiparva

name: Even the Worst Sinner Is Saved by Devotion
proposition: Even the most sinful person who worships with undivided devotion should be considered righteous — my devotee does not perish
repetition_rationale: The radical soteriology of devotion — overcoming all karmic weight.
echo_hypothesis: Redemption through devotion stories in MBH

name: All Beings Can Attain the Highest Goal
proposition: Even those of sinful birth — women, vaishyas, shudras — taking refuge in the divine, they too attain the highest goal
repetition_rationale: The universalism of liberation — across all social strata.
echo_hypothesis: Liberation across social classes in Shantiparva — stories of shudras and women attaining moksha

name: The Divine Is the Origin of Gods and Great Seers
proposition: The seven great seers and the four Manus were born from the divine's mind — the world's progeny descend from them
repetition_rationale: The genealogy of cosmic authority — all teachers trace to the divine.
echo_hypothesis: Seven sages and Manu lineages in MBH — Adiparva genealogies

name: All Qualities and States Arise from the Divine
proposition: Intelligence, knowledge, freedom from delusion, patience, truth — all arise from the divine alone
repetition_rationale: All categories — even opposites — originate from the divine. Anticipates vibhuti list.
echo_hypothesis: Passages attributing all virtues to divine origin in Shantiparva

name: The Divine Is the Self in the Heart — Beginning Middle End
proposition: The divine is the self abiding in the heart of all beings; the beginning, middle, and end of all beings
repetition_rationale: The triple formula (adi-madhya-anta) is the most concentrated ekam sat formulation.
echo_hypothesis: Adi-madhya-anta formula as divine epithet in MBH hymns

name: Vibhutis — Manifest Excellence as Divine Fragment
proposition: Whatever being has glory, grace, or power — know that to have sprung from a fragment of the divine's splendor; the divine pervades the world with a single portion
repetition_rationale: The entire vibhuti catalogue is one extended claim: excellence anywhere is a vibhuti.
echo_hypothesis: Vibhuti lists and divine excellence enumerations in MBH

name: The Divine Gives Liberating Wisdom to Devotees
proposition: For those constantly united in devotion, the divine gives the yoga of understanding and destroys the darkness of ignorance with the lamp of knowledge
repetition_rationale: Divine grace as the final cause of liberating knowledge.
echo_hypothesis: Divine grace enabling liberation in MBH — passages where divine intervention brings sudden clarity

name: Vishvarupa — The Entire Universe in One Body
proposition: Arjuna beholds in the divine body the entire world standing as one — all creation gathered at one point
repetition_rationale: A direct vision rather than inferential teaching. The visual datum for ekam sat.
echo_hypothesis: Cosmic vision episodes in MBH — Sanjaya's vision, Narada's vision of Narayana

name: Divya Drishti — Only Devotion Reveals the Cosmic Form
proposition: The divine form cannot be seen with ordinary eyes; only by undivided devotion can it be known, seen, and entered
repetition_rationale: The epistemology of bhakti: only love reveals what intellect and austerity cannot.
echo_hypothesis: Passages on special vision given by grace in MBH — Sanjaya's divya-drishti

name: Kala — The Divine as Time the Destroyer
proposition: The divine reveals itself as Time the destroyer — even without Arjuna, all warriors shall not exist; Arjuna is merely the instrument
repetition_rationale: The nimittamatra (mere instrument) claim reframes all human agency.
echo_hypothesis: Kala-as-divine-destroyer passages in MBH — Dhritarashtra's laments, Markandeya's kala teachings

name: Terror and Devotion — Seeking the Familiar Form
proposition: Having seen the terrifying cosmic form, Arjuna begs to see the familiar form — the devotee needs an approachable form
repetition_rationale: The dialectic of the cosmic and the intimate: absolute terror answered by the comfort of the personal form.
echo_hypothesis: Terror-to-comfort devotion arcs in MBH

name: Saguna Bhakti Is Easier for the Embodied
proposition: Those who worship the formless imperishable face greater hardship; the path through the personal divine is easier for embodied beings
repetition_rationale: The pragmatic hierarchy: saguna over nirguna for embodied seekers.
echo_hypothesis: Debates on saguna vs nirguna paths in Shantiparva

name: Graduated Path — From Mind-Fixing to Fruit-Renunciation
proposition: If you cannot fix your mind, try abhyasa-yoga; if that fails, act for the divine; if that fails, renounce fruits; even this gives peace
repetition_rationale: The pedagogical descending ladder — BG's practical concession to different capacities.
echo_hypothesis: Graded instruction passages in MBH

name: Fruit-Renunciation Surpasses Meditation
proposition: Knowledge is better than practice; meditation better than knowledge; renunciation of fruit is better than meditation — from renunciation peace immediately follows
repetition_rationale: A complete comparative ranking in a single verse.
echo_hypothesis: Comparative hierarchies of spiritual practices in Shantiparva

name: Portrait of the Ideal Devotee
proposition: The ideal devotee is free of hatred, friendly, compassionate, without ego or possessiveness, equal in pain and pleasure, patient, content, self-restrained — such a one is supremely dear to the divine
repetition_rationale: The most extensive character portrait in BG — 8 verses defining the ideal devotee.
echo_hypothesis: Character descriptions of ideal sages in MBH — Bhishma's deathbed portrait of the wise
